items.title: Activation module
items.question: Does the perturbation screen recover known T-cell signaling without priors?
items.readout: 245 genes are quiet at Rest and become broad regulators after stimulation.
items.takeaway: The screen recovers the TCR activation cascade from perturbation effects alone.

items.title: Regulator versus effector
items.question: Do famous checkpoint and cytokine genes drive the transcriptome in this assay?
items.readout: 18 field-targeted genes have confirmed knockdown and near-zero stimulated DE.
items.takeaway: Important targets are often outputs, not transcriptional drivers here.

items.title: Reach is not regulation
items.question: What does a naive effect-size ranking mistake for immune biology?
items.readout: 139 high-Rest-reach genes mark general machinery rather than T-cell-specific regulation.
items.takeaway: Rest reach separates housekeeping artifacts from activation-specific regulators.

items.title: Verifier transfer
items.question: Does the same checker interface separate housekeeping from immune-specific effects?
items.readout: Essentiality genes replicate in K562 and RPE1; the activation module stays inert.
items.takeaway: A second cell type confirms the housekeeping versus T-cell-specific split.

items.title: Regulon recovery
items.question: Can perturbation alone recover known TF target biology?
items.readout: CollecTRI targets are 4.03x enriched among moved genes, with combined p near 1e-26.
items.takeaway: Known regulons reappear from the data, and sign disagreements become citable terrain.
